data.content: I can see your project — an MSTest project with a failing test (`CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00`). Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on save.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project. They want to use hot reload to iterate on failing tests. I can set up `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can see the output and iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-97c19f1d64564f6990793a19dcd99136 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.content: There's a missing `using System;` — let me fix that so the watcher can compile and run.
data.reasoningText: There's a compilation error - `ArgumentException` needs `System` namespace. Let me fix that and the failing test will then show. Let me add `using System;` to the file.
